original: For years, studies have found that first-generation college students—those who do not have a parent with a college degree—lag other students on a range of education achievement factors.
translation_literal: 多年来，研究发现，第一代大学生，也就是父母没有大学学位的学生，在一系列教育成就因素上落后于其他学生。

chunks.english: that first-generation college students lag other students on a range of education achievement factors
chunks.chinese: 第一代大学生在一系列教育成就因素上落后于其他学生
chunks.relation: 作 found 的宾语

chunks.english: those who do not have a parent with a college degree
chunks.chinese: 父母没有大学学位的那些学生
chunks.relation: 解释 first-generation college students
宾语从句内部主语
宾语从句内部谓语
作 lag 的宾语

sentence_patterns.explanation: those who... 是 first-generation college students 的同位语解释。

original: Their grades are lower and their dropout rates are higher.
translation_literal: 他们的成绩更低，辍学率更高。

chunks.english: and their dropout rates are higher
chunks.chinese: 并且他们的辍学率更高
chunks.relation: 与前一分句并列

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: A are lower and B are higher
sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个并列判断共同说明第一代大学生处境不利。

original: But since such students are most likely to advance economically if they succeed in higher education, colleges and universities have pushed for decades to recruit more of them.
translation_literal: 但是，由于这样的学生如果在高等教育中成功，就最有可能在经济上取得进步，学院和大学几十年来一直努力招收更多这样的学生。

chunks.english: since such students are most likely to advance economically if they succeed in higher education
chunks.chinese: 由于这样的学生如果在高等教育中成功就最有可能在经济上取得进步

chunks.english: if they succeed in higher education
chunks.chinese: 如果他们在高等教育中成功
chunks.relation: 说明 advance 的条件
说明持续时间
说明推动的目标
since 原因状语从句内部主语
since 原因状语从句内部谓语

original: This has created “a paradox” in that recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail, means that higher education has “continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close” an achievement gap based on social class, according to the depressing beginning of a paper forthcoming in the journal Psychological Science.
translation_literal: 这造成了“一个悖论”，因为招收第一代学生、但随后看着他们中的许多人失败，意味着高等教育“继续复制并扩大，而不是缩小”一个基于社会阶层的成就差距，正如即将发表于《心理科学》期刊的一篇论文令人沮丧的开头所说。

chunks.relation: 作 created 的宾语

chunks.english: in that recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail, means that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen an achievement gap
chunks.chinese: 因为招收第一代学生但随后看着他们失败，意味着高等教育继续复制并扩大一个成就差距
chunks.relation: 说明造成悖论的原因

chunks.english: recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail
chunks.chinese: 招收第一代学生但随后看着他们中的许多人失败
chunks.relation: 状语从句「in that recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail, means that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen an achievement gap」中的主语部分

chunks.english: that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close, an achievement gap based on social class
chunks.chinese: 高等教育继续复制并扩大而不是缩小一个基于社会阶层的成就差距
chunks.relation: 作 means 的宾语
插入说明与 reproduce and widen 对比

chunks.english: according to the depressing beginning of a paper forthcoming in the journal Psychological Science
chunks.chinese: 根据即将发表于《心理科学》期刊的一篇论文令人沮丧的开头
chunks.relation: 说明观点来源
主语「recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail」中的主语部分

chunks.chinese: 看着他们中的许多人失败
chunks.relation: 主语「recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail」中的谓语部分
状语从句「in that recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail, means that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen an achievement gap」中的谓语部分
宾语从句「that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close, an achievement gap based on social class」内部主语

chunks.english: has continued to reproduce and widen
chunks.relation: 宾语从句「that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close, an achievement gap based on social class」内部谓语

chunks.english: an achievement gap based on social class
chunks.chinese: 基于社会阶层的成就差距
chunks.relation: 宾语从句「that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close, an achievement gap based on social class」内部宾语，作用于「that higher education has continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close, an achievement gap based on social class」

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 动词短语中插入对比成分
sentence_patterns.explanation: rather than close 插在 reproduce and widen 与宾语之间，阅读时要与 continued to reproduce and widen 对照。

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语较长，谓语与宾语从句相隔
sentence_patterns.explanation: recruiting...but then watching... 是较长主语，真正谓语是 means。

original: But the article is actually quite optimistic, as it outlines a potential solution to this problem, suggesting that an approach (which involves a one-hour, next-to-no-cost program) can close 63 percent of the achievement gap (measured by such factors as grades) between first-generation and other students.
translation_literal: 但是这篇文章实际上相当乐观，因为它概述了这个问题的一个潜在解决方案，表明一种方法（包括一个一小时、几乎无成本的项目）可以缩小第一代学生和其他学生之间63%的成就差距（由成绩等因素衡量）。

chunks.relation: 作 is 的表语

chunks.english: as it outlines a potential solution to this problem
chunks.chinese: 因为它概述了这个问题的一个潜在解决方案
chunks.relation: 说明乐观的原因

chunks.english: suggesting that an approach can close 63 percent of the achievement gap
chunks.chinese: 表明一种方法可以缩小63%的成就差距
chunks.relation: 补充说明 article 的内容

chunks.english: which involves a one-hour, next-to-no-cost program
chunks.chinese: 它包括一个一小时、几乎无成本的项目
chunks.relation: 修饰 approach

chunks.english: measured by such factors as grades
chunks.relation: 补充说明 achievement gap
原因状语从句「as it outlines a potential solution to this problem」内部主语
原因状语从句「as it outlines a potential solution to this problem」内部谓语

chunks.english: a potential solution to this problem
chunks.chinese: 这个问题的潜在解决方案
chunks.relation: 原因状语从句「as it outlines a potential solution to this problem」内部宾语，作用于「as it outlines a potential solution to this problem」
状语「suggesting that an approach can close 63 percent of the achievement gap」内部主语
状语「suggesting that an approach can close 63 percent of the achievement gap」内部谓语
状语「suggesting that an approach can close 63 percent of the achievement gap」内部宾语，作用于「suggesting that an approach can close 63 percent of the achievement gap」

sentence_patterns.explanation: as 在这里引导原因结构，说明文章乐观的原因。
which involves... 和 measured by... 都以括号形式补充关键信息。

original: The authors of the paper are from different universities, and their findings are based on a study involving 147 students (who completed the project) at an unnamed private university.
translation_literal: 这篇论文的作者来自不同大学，并且他们的发现基于一项涉及一所未具名私立大学147名学生（他们完成了该项目）的研究。

chunks.english: The authors of the paper are from different universities
chunks.chinese: 这篇论文的作者来自不同大学

chunks.english: and their findings are based on a study involving 147 students at an unnamed private university
chunks.chinese: 并且他们的发现基于一项涉及一所未具名私立大学147名学生的研究
chunks.relation: 与前一分句并列
修饰 study
修饰 students

chunks.chinese: 在一所未具名私立大学
chunks.relation: 说明研究对象所在学校
第一并列分句内部主语
第一并列分句内部谓语
第二并列分句内部主语
第二并列分句内部谓语

sentence_patterns.explanation: who completed the project 用括号补充说明 students。

original: First generation was defined as not having a parent with a four-year college degree.
translation_literal: 第一代被定义为没有父母拥有四年制大学学位。

chunks.english: not having a parent with a four-year college degree
chunks.chinese: 没有父母拥有四年制大学学位
chunks.relation: 作 define as 的内容

original: Most of the first-generation students (59.1 percent) were recipients of Pell Grants, a federal grant for undergraduates with financial need, while this was true only for 8.6 percent of the students with at least one parent with a four-year degree.
translation_literal: 大多数第一代学生（59.1%）是佩尔助学金的获得者，这是一种面向有经济需求的本科生的联邦助学金，而至少有一位父母拥有四年制学位的学生中只有8.6%是这样。

chunks.relation: 补充比例
作 were 的表语

chunks.english: a federal grant for undergraduates with financial need
chunks.chinese: 一种面向有经济需求的本科生的联邦助学金
chunks.relation: 解释 Pell Grants

chunks.english: while this was true only for 8.6 percent of the students with at least one parent with a four-year degree
chunks.chinese: 而至少有一位父母拥有四年制学位的学生中只有8.6%是这样
chunks.relation: 形成对比
while 对比状语从句内部主语
while 对比状语从句内部谓语

chunks.english: true only for 8.6 percent of the students
chunks.relation: while 对比状语从句内部表语

sentence_patterns.explanation: a federal grant... 解释 Pell Grants。
59.1 percent 插入主语后，补充具体比例。

original: Their thesis—that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact—was based on the view that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge about how to deal with the issues that face most college students.
translation_literal: 他们的论点，即一个相对适度的干预可能产生重大影响，是基于这样一种观点：第一代学生最缺乏的可能不是潜力，而是关于如何处理大多数大学生都会面临的问题的实践知识。

chunks.english: that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact
chunks.chinese: 即一个相对适度的干预可能产生重大影响
chunks.relation: 解释 thesis
作 based on 的宾语

chunks.english: that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge
chunks.chinese: 第一代学生最缺乏的可能不是潜力而是实践知识
chunks.relation: 解释 the view

chunks.english: how to deal with the issues that face most college students
chunks.chinese: 如何处理大多数大学生都会面临的问题
chunks.relation: 作 about 的宾语

chunks.chinese: 大多数大学生都会面临的
chunks.relation: 修饰 issues
同位语解释结构「that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact」内部主语
同位语解释结构「that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact」内部谓语
同位语解释结构「that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact」内部宾语，作用于「that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact」
同位语从句「that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge」内部主语
同位语从句「that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge」内部谓语

同位语从句「that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge」中的宾语部分，作用于「that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge」

同位语从句「that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge」中的宾语部分，作用于「that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge」

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 名词 + 破折号解释 / that 从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: 破折号中的 that 从句解释 thesis，后面的 that 从句解释 the view。

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语与谓语被同位语隔开
sentence_patterns.explanation: Their thesis 与 was based on 被破折号同位语隔开，阅读时要先合并主干。

key_phrases.meaning: 不是在……方面，而是在……方面

original: They cite past research by several authors to show that this is the gap that must be narrowed to close the achievement gap.
translation_literal: 他们引用几位作者过去的研究来表明，这就是必须被缩小以弥合成就差距的差距。

chunks.relation: 作 cite 的宾语

chunks.english: to show that this is the gap that must be narrowed to close the achievement gap
chunks.chinese: 来表明这就是必须被缩小以弥合成就差距的差距
chunks.relation: 说明引用研究的目的

chunks.english: that this is the gap that must be narrowed to close the achievement gap
chunks.chinese: 这就是必须被缩小以弥合成就差距的差距
chunks.relation: 作 show 的宾语

chunks.english: that must be narrowed to close the achievement gap
chunks.chinese: 必须被缩小以弥合成就差距的
chunks.relation: 修饰 gap
show 后宾语从句内部主语
show 后宾语从句内部谓语
show 后宾语从句内部表语
定语从句内部主语，指 gap
定语从句内部谓语

original: Many first-generation students “struggle to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the ‘rules of the game,’ and take advantage of college resources,” they write.
translation_literal: 他们写道，许多第一代学生“努力适应高等教育中的中产阶级文化，学习‘游戏规则’，并利用大学资源”。

chunks.english: to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources
chunks.chinese: 适应高等教育中的中产阶级文化、学习游戏规则并利用大学资源
chunks.relation: 作 struggle 的内容
说明引语来源

chunks.english: to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education
chunks.chinese: 适应高等教育中的中产阶级文化
chunks.relation: 宾语「to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources」中的宾语部分，作用于「to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources」

chunks.english: learn the rules of the game
chunks.relation: 宾语「to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources」中的宾语部分，作用于「to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources」

宾语「to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources」中的宾语部分，作用于「to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the rules of the game, and take advantage of college resources」

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: to do A, do B, and do C
sentence_patterns.explanation: navigate, learn, take advantage of 三个动作并列，都是 first-generation students struggle to do 的内容。
they write 说明前面引语来自论文作者。

original: And this becomes more of a problem when colleges don’t talk about the class advantages and disadvantages of different groups of students.
translation_literal: 当大学不谈论不同学生群体的阶层优势和劣势时，这会变得更像一个问题。

chunks.relation: 作 becomes 的表语

chunks.english: when colleges don’t talk about the class advantages and disadvantages of different groups of students
chunks.chinese: 当大学不谈论不同学生群体的阶层优势和劣势时
chunks.relation: 说明问题加重的条件
when 状语从句内部主语
when 状语从句内部谓语

chunks.english: the class advantages and disadvantages of different groups of students
chunks.chinese: 不同学生群体的阶层优势和劣势
chunks.relation: 作 talk about 的宾语

original: “Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences, many first-generation students lack sight about why they are struggling and do not understand how students ‘like them’ can improve.”
translation_literal: “因为美国的学院和大学很少承认社会阶层如何影响学生的教育经历，许多第一代学生看不清他们为什么在挣扎，也不理解像他们这样的学生如何能够提高。”

chunks.english: Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences
chunks.chinese: 因为美国的学院和大学很少承认社会阶层如何影响学生的教育经历

chunks.relation: 主句第一个并列谓语

chunks.chinese: 关于他们为什么在挣扎
chunks.relation: 作 about 的宾语
主句第二个并列谓语，与 lack sight 并列

chunks.english: how students like them can improve
chunks.chinese: 像他们这样的学生如何能够提高
chunks.relation: 作 understand 的宾语
原因状语从句「Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences」内部主语
原因状语从句「Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences」内部谓语

chunks.english: how social class can affect students’ educational experiences
chunks.chinese: 社会阶层如何影响学生的教育经历
chunks.relation: 原因状语从句「Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences」内部宾语，作用于「Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences」

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 谓语 A and 谓语 B
sentence_patterns.explanation: lack sight 和 do not understand 并列，共同说明第一代学生的困境。
